title: cuscuta foetida
intro: Cuscuta foetida, also known as Dodder or Convolvulus, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and absorbing their nutrients. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta foetida include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta foetida is often used in the form of powder or decoction, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
